title: White nights, and other stories
author: Dostoyevsky, Fyodor
summary: It was a youthful night in Petersburg when a solitary dreamer, long acquainted with the city and its habitual faces, feels crushed by despondency as residents depart for summer villas. He paces grimy rooms and embankments, noticing houses as friends, lamenting a pretty pink house painted yellow. Relieved to reach the fields and meadows, he returns intoxicated by spring. On the canal embankment he discovers a dark girl in a yellow hat weeping; she is pursued by a staggering, obtrusive gentleman. Timid yet courageous, the narrator intervenes with his knotted stick, thereby repelling the man and rescuing the grateful girl

characters.name: The Dreamer (narrator)
characters.personality: solitary, romantic, timid but compassionate, observant of Petersburg
characters.lifespan: Remains in Petersburg; wanders the city and countryside, intervenes to save the weeping girl in the excerpt

characters.name: Matrona
characters.lifespan: Remains the narrator's maid; ignores admonitions and leaves the spider's web untouched

characters.name: Old man (Fontanka acquaintance)
characters.personality: grave, pensive, habitual in his daily routine
characters.lifespan: Continues his daily walks at the Fontanka and exchanges mutual acknowledgment with the narrator

characters.name: The pretty little house
characters.personality: proud, hospitable (as perceived by the narrator)
characters.lifespan: Beloved by the narrator but is later painted yellow, causing his dismay

characters.name: Girl in the yellow hat (unknown lady)
characters.lifespan: Found weeping on the embankment, pursued, and rescued by the narrator; remains grateful (further fate not described in excerpt)

characters.name: Staggering gentleman
characters.lifespan: Pursues the girl; is frightened off by the narrator's knotted stick

characters.name: Waggoners
characters.lifespan: Transport household goods in caravans to summer villas; multiply in the narrator's imagination

characters.name: Decrepit cook
characters.lifespan: Perches atop the household goods during removal to the country; part of the caravans

characters.name: Street-seller (flower vendor)
characters.personality: ornamental vendor, part of street life
characters.lifespan: Sells pots of flowers to households preparing to move to summer villas

unusual_words.definition: given to sudden changes of mood or behavior
unusual_words.context: Describes ‘capricious and ill-humoured people’ under the starry sky

unusual_words.definition: a state of low spirits from loss of hope or courage
unusual_words.context: The narrator says he had been oppressed by a strange despondency all that day

unusual_words.definition: abandoning or leaving someone
unusual_words.context: He felt that every one was forsaking him and going away

unusual_words.definition: a country house or summer residence
unusual_words.context: Residents depart for their summer villas; narrator envies them

unusual_words.definition: a person one knows slightly; familiarity
unusual_words.context: He remarks he had hardly an acquaintance after living eight years

unusual_words.definition: short for Saint Petersburg, the city setting
unusual_words.context: The narrator has been living almost eight years in Petersburg

unusual_words.definition: a river/embankment in Saint Petersburg
unusual_words.context: He meets an old man every day at the same hour in Fontanka

unusual_words.definition: speaking very softly
unusual_words.context: The old man is always whispering to himself

unusual_words.definition: waving or flourishing (an object) as a threat or in excitement
unusual_words.context: The old man brandishes his left arm as he walks

unusual_words.definition: knobbly, rough, and twisted, especially with age
unusual_words.context: The old man holds a long gnarled stick with a gold knob

unusual_words.definition: a rounded handle or ornament
unusual_words.context: The gnarled stick is topped with a gold knob

unusual_words.definition: a sad and depressed state
unusual_words.context: He wandered about the town in profound dejection

unusual_words.definition: inability to deal with or understand something; puzzlement
unusual_words.context: In perplexity he scanned his grimy green walls for the cause of his uneasiness

unusual_words.definition: covered with or characterized by grime; dirty
unusual_words.context: He inspected his grimy green walls

unusual_words.definition: slovenly or untidy behavior (archaic/uncommon usage)
unusual_words.context: He scolds Matrona about the spider's web and sluttishness in general

unusual_words.definition: an eight-legged arachnid; used in phrase 'spider's web'
unusual_words.context: He notes the spider's web hanging from his ceiling

unusual_words.definition: a network of fine threads spun by a spider
unusual_words.context: The spider's web is comfortably hanging in its place

unusual_words.definition: the name of the servant in the narrator's lodging
unusual_words.context: He sent for Matrona and gave her fatherly admonitions

unusual_words.definition: sounding sad and mournful
unusual_words.context: The little pink house seemed to cry plaintive, 'They are painting me yellow!'

unusual_words.definition: people perceived as uncivilized or crude
unusual_words.context: He calls those who painted the house yellow 'villains, barbarians!'

unusual_words.definition: decorative molding at the top of a building wall
unusual_words.context: They spared nothing, neither columns, nor cornices, when they painted

unusual_words.definition: suffering from or causing a liver-related illness; here figuratively 'sickening'
unusual_words.context: The change of the house's color almost made him bilious

unusual_words.definition: decorated again or anew
unusual_words.context: One house informed him it is being redecorated to-morrow

unusual_words.definition: a wall or bank of earth or stone built to prevent a river flooding; riverfront promenade
unusual_words.context: He walks along the canal embankment where at that hour you never meet a soul

unusual_words.definition: short for Kamenny Island, a Petersburg locality
unusual_words.context: He distinguishes inhabitants of Kamenny Island by their studied elegance

unusual_words.definition: short for Aptekarsky Island, a Petersburg area
unusual_words.context: He notes Aptekarsky Island visitors among summer-bound residents

unusual_words.definition: a suburb/imperial estate outside Saint Petersburg
unusual_words.context: He notes people who visit the Peterhof Road when recognizing summer travellers

unusual_words.definition: a rural locality outside Saint Petersburg
unusual_words.context: Visitors to Pargolovo impressed one by their reasonable and dignified air

unusual_words.definition: Krestovsky Island, a recreational area of Petersburg
unusual_words.context: A tripper to Krestovsky Island could be recognized by irrepressible gaiety

unusual_words.definition: not able to be controlled or restrained
unusual_words.context: He recognizes Krestovsky trippers by a look of irrepressible gaiety

unusual_words.definition: the state or quality of being light-hearted or cheerful
unusual_words.context: Irrepressible gaiety marks the tripper to Krestovsky Island

unusual_words.definition: drivers of wagons (archaic/spelled with double 'g')
unusual_words.context: Long processions of waggoners walk with waggons loaded with furniture

unusual_words.definition: worn out or ruined because of age or neglect
unusual_words.context: Frequently with a decrepit cook sitting on the top of it all

unusual_words.definition: tools or implements, especially for the kitchen
unusual_words.context: Waggons loaded with domestic utensils of all sorts

unusual_words.definition: groups of vehicles or people traveling together
unusual_words.context: Everything was going in regular caravans to the summer villas

unusual_words.definition: fields of grassland used for hay or grazing
unusual_words.context: He passed the barrier and walked between cultivated fields and meadows

unusual_words.definition: rolled bundles of dried and fermented tobacco leaves
unusual_words.context: All the passers-by gave him such friendly looks; they were all smoking cigars

unusual_words.definition: country used here as a simile for warmth and freedom
unusual_words.context: He says it was as though he had suddenly found himself in Italy

unusual_words.definition: affected by consumption (tuberculosis); frail and wasting
unusual_words.context: He compares the spring revival to a frail, consumptive girl becoming lovely

unusual_words.definition: physically weak or delicate
unusual_words.context: A frail, consumptive girl at whom one sometimes looks with compassion

unusual_words.definition: in a way that cannot be explained
unusual_words.context: She becomes inexplicably lovely and exquisite for an instant

unusual_words.definition: extremely beautiful and delicate
unusual_words.context: The girl's momentary beauty is described as exquisite

unusual_words.definition: the chest or breast; figuratively the seat of emotions
unusual_words.context: He imagines people moving to the bosom of their family at the villa

unusual_words.definition: moving up and down with effort or emotion
unusual_words.context: What set that bosom heaving? he asks

unusual_words.definition: called forth or brought about
unusual_words.context: What summoned the blood to those pale, wan cheeks?

unusual_words.definition: pale and giving the impression of illness or exhaustion
unusual_words.context: He mentions pale, wan cheeks when describing the girl

unusual_words.definition: severe mental or physical pain or suffering
unusual_words.context: Traces of a mortal anguish and regret for the fleeting distraction

unusual_words.definition: deep regret or guilt for a wrong committed
unusual_words.context: Even signs of remorse appear in the girl's demeanor

unusual_words.definition: deceptively or dangerously
unusual_words.context: He laments that the momentary beauty flashed so treacherously

unusual_words.definition: in great suspense or anxious anticipation (archaic)
unusual_words.context: He walked along singing with bated breath when happy

unusual_words.definition: made less loud or clear; muted
unusual_words.context: He heard a muffled sob from the girl at the embankment

unusual_words.definition: a convulsive catch in the breath, as from grief
unusual_words.context: The muffled sobs reveal that the girl is crying

unusual_words.definition: having or expressing a lively, cheerful, and self-confident manner
unusual_words.context: She wore a very charming yellow hat and a jaunty little black mantle

unusual_words.definition: a cloak or shawl; here a small coat
unusual_words.context: The girl wore a jaunty little black mantle

unusual_words.definition: discovering or perceiving by intuition
unusual_words.context: She, divining this, left the embankment and crossed the road

unusual_words.definition: a high-pitched piercing cry
unusual_words.context: The girl uttered a shriek when overtaken by the gentleman

unusual_words.definition: tied or formed into a knot; used to describe the stick
unusual_words.context: The narrator blesses his luck for the excellent knotted stick

unusual_words.definition: too attractive and tempting to be resisted
unusual_words.context: The knotted stick served as the irresistible argument to deter the man

unusual_words.definition: a reason or justification; here used ironically for the stick's force
unusual_words.context: He calls the stick an irresistible argument

unusual_words.definition: so frightened that one is unable to move; stunned
unusual_words.context: He stopped as though petrified when he first saw the girl

unusual_words.definition: moving or stirring rapidly and lightly; trembling
unusual_words.context: His heart was fluttering like a captured bird

unusual_words.definition: taken or ensnared; metaphorical use for the heart
unusual_words.context: His heart fluttered like a captured bird

unusual_words.definition: walking or moving unsteadily, as if about to fall
unusual_words.context: A gentleman in evening dress was staggering and leaning against the wall

unusual_words.definition: having or showing a composed or serious manner worthy of respect
unusual_words.context: The gentleman was of dignified years though not dignified in carriage

unusual_words.definition: a polite form of address for a woman
unusual_words.context: He reflects on how he might have addressed her: 'Madam!'

unusual_words.definition: short for Nevsky Prospekt, a principal avenue in Petersburg
unusual_words.context: He mentions walking in the Nevsky as part of his wanderings

unusual_words.definition: a physical obstruction or gate; here the city gates
unusual_words.context: He found himself at the city gates and passed the barrier into fields

unusual_words.definition: prepared and used for raising crops; here orderly or farmed
unusual_words.context: He walked between cultivated fields and meadows

unusual_words.definition: cheerful and carefree
unusual_words.context: After passing the city gates he felt lighthearted

unusual_words.definition: moving slowly and laboriously
unusual_words.context: Boats heavily loaded were crawling along the Neva or Fontanka

unusual_words.definition: public gardens or parks in the city (capitalized in text)
unusual_words.context: He mentions going to the Gardens among his daily walks

unusual_words.definition: small sparkling objects or decorations, used figuratively

unusual_words.definition: light in color or lacking in vitality
unusual_words.context: He notes the girl's pale face and wan cheeks

unusual_words.definition: showing a lack of courage or confidence; easily frightened

unusual_words.definition: quiet, gentle, and easily imposed on; submissive

unusual_words.definition: overcome with delight or excitement; metaphorically 'drunk' on feeling

unusual_words.definition: examination or close observation
unusual_words.context: He had almost made a study of the faces of the people he met

unusual_words.definition: vertical pillars used architecturally
unusual_words.context: They spared nothing, neither columns, nor cornices, when painting

unusual_words.definition: awkward or clumsy in appearance

unusual_words.definition: in a manner showing welcome or friendliness
unusual_words.context: The narrator felt the house looked at him hospitably

unusual_words.definition: causing one to be puzzled or perplexed
unusual_words.context: For two evenings he was puzzling his brains to think what was amiss

unusual_words.definition: authoritative counsel or warning
unusual_words.context: He gave Matrona some fatherly admonitions about the spider's web

unusual_words.definition: looked fixedly or vacantly at someone or something
unusual_words.context: Matrona simply stared at him in amazement

unusual_words.definition: a single sheet of glass in a window
unusual_words.context: Delicate fingers had tapped upon the pane as a head was thrust out

unusual_words.definition: a vendor who sells goods on the street
unusual_words.context: A pretty girl called to a street-seller with pots of flowers

unusual_words.definition: containers, here used for potted flowers
unusual_words.context: She called to a street-seller with pots of flowers

unusual_words.definition: floral plants sold for decoration or pleasure
unusual_words.context: People bought flowers to take with them to summer villas

unusual_words.definition: uncomfortably warm or lacking fresh air; cramped
unusual_words.context: The narrator thinks people won't enjoy flowers in stuffy town lodgings

unusual_words.definition: characteristic of, influenced by, or representing a current popular style

unusual_words.definition: a person making a short journey for pleasure
unusual_words.context: A tripper to Krestovsky Island could be recognized by his look

unusual_words.definition: identified from having seen before
unusual_words.context: He could be recognized at first sight by his manner

unusual_words.definition: a feeling of anxiety or discomfort
unusual_words.context: He had been feeling a strange uneasiness for days

unusual_words.definition: at once; immediately
unusual_words.context: When he reached the city gates, instantly he felt lighthearted

unusual_words.definition: pulsing or beating strongly and regularly
unusual_words.context: He walked with bated breath and a loudly throbbing heart

unusual_words.definition: to offer oneself or one's services freely
unusual_words.context: Girls flee to avoid anyone who might volunteer to accompany them home

unusual_words.definition: noticeable or prominent in an unwelcome way
unusual_words.context: The narrator describes the man as the obtrusive gentleman who pursued her
